question: What is CSA Loom?
expectedAnswer: CSA Loom is the Cloud Scale Analytics platform that delivers the Microsoft Fabric experience inside any Azure tenant where Fabric is not yet available — a push-button deployment, a Fluent UI v9 console mirroring the Fabric workspace layout, a conversational Setup Wizard, and parity services, all running on Azure-native services with no Fabric dependency.

question: Does CSA Loom require a Microsoft Fabric capacity or workspace to run?
expectedAnswer: No. Every Loom item type maps to an Azure-native backend (lakehouse to ADLS Gen2 + Delta, KQL database to ADX, eventstream to Event Hubs + Stream Analytics, and so on). Fabric is strictly opt-in, never required — no Fabric capacity or workspace is needed.
mustNotMention: you must bind a Fabric workspace

question: Which Azure service backs a lakehouse by default in Loom?
expectedAnswer: The default lakehouse backend is ADLS Gen2 with Delta tables; T-SQL queries run through Synapse Serverless. No OneLake or Fabric capacity is involved on the default path.

question: How long does a from-scratch CSA Loom deployment take, and what are the main steps?
expectedAnswer: About 90 minutes end to end: clone + authenticate, create the Entra group for Loom Admins, deploy the infrastructure (40-90 min), build the app images and bring up the Container Apps (15-25 min), then run the post-deploy bootstrap (10-15 min) which is required to sign in, and finally verify the Console.

question: Why can't the Container Apps come up in a single bicep deployment against a brand-new ACR?
expectedAnswer: A fresh ACR is created empty, so Container Apps referencing loom images in it cannot pull anything yet. The image build is a required second phase: run the full-app-deploy workflow, which builds and pushes every app image into the ACR server-side and then brings the Container Apps up on those images.

question: I deployed Loom but nobody can sign in to the Console. What did I most likely miss?
expectedAnswer: Skipping the post-deploy bootstrap is the most common cause of a deployed-but-can't-sign-in Console. The bootstrap wires sign-in (the MSAL app registration) and the data-plane grants; run the post-deploy bootstrap workflow with region, admin subscription, and boundary set.

question: What availability model does a default Loom deployment give me?
expectedAnswer: A single-region deployment with zone redundancy: the data lake is zone-redundant (Standard_ZRS), Cosmos runs continuous backup with 7-day point-in-time restore, and apps are stateless containers. A zone outage is survived transparently; a full region loss is not auto-covered by default — cross-region survivability is an explicit opt-in.

question: What does an F8 single-sub Commercial deployment cost per month?
expectedAnswer: Roughly $3-5K/month of Azure consumption under active use, with an idle floor around $1-2K/month because APIM, Container Apps, AI Search, Key Vault, Front Door, and Purview bill continuously. Pause Databricks and ADX from Admin > Capacity to cut active cost.

question: How do I tear down a Loom deployment and redeploy under the same names?
expectedAnswer: Delete the deployment's resource groups (admin RG and DLZ RG), then before redeploying under the same names purge the soft-deleted Key Vault, Cognitive/OpenAI, and APIM resources, since they block name reuse.

question: How do configuration gates work in CSA Loom?
expectedAnswer: Per-client ConfigGate helpers return the missing env var (or null when configured); BFF routes translate a non-null gate into a 503 with a not_configured code; the UI renders an honest warning MessageBar naming the exact env var, role, or bicep module. The declarative source of truth is ENV_CHECKS in lib/admin/self-audit.ts.

question: Where can I see every configuration gate and fix it in one place?
expectedAnswer: The /admin/gates page lists every gate with live status, filters, and one-click Fix-it wizards; GET /api/admin/gates evaluates every gate in one call, and Fix-it applies through the same audited env-config write path. Copilot can also list, explain, and resolve gates.

question: How long does applying an env var via a gate Fix-it wizard take to become live?
expectedAnswer: Applying a value rolls a new container revision, which takes about 1-2 minutes. The wizard says so honestly and re-probes the gate until the revision lands — it never fakes an instant flip.

question: Which cloud boundaries does Loom support and on what container compute?
expectedAnswer: Azure Commercial and GCC run on Container Apps with Databricks Unity Catalog + Purview overlay; FedRAMP High / GCC-High / IL4 run on AKS with Purview as the primary catalog; DoD IL5 runs on AKS with Apache Atlas as the catalog.

question: What happens to my Loom investment when Microsoft Fabric reaches my audit boundary?
expectedAnswer: It ports forward 1:1 — Delta tables become OneLake shortcuts, dbt models run unchanged, TMDL semantic models are identical, and KQL queries are identical. Loom is the bridge to Fabric, not a trap.

question: What is the difference between the Admin Plane, a Data Landing Zone, and a workspace?
expectedAnswer: The Admin Plane is one subscription per organization hosting the Console, MCP server, Copilot, shared catalog, and shared ADX cluster. A Data Landing Zone (DLZ) is one subscription per domain/agency whose spoke VNet peers to the Admin Plane hub. A workspace is a data product inside a DLZ — a resource group holding Loom items.

question: Where is the one-time tenant bootstrap configuration documented?
expectedAnswer: docs/fiab/v3-tenant-bootstrap.md is the canonical post-deploy one-time-config reference: day-one operator prerequisites, per-feature grants, env var reference, and bicep-sync notes for each capability.

question: What does the Loom Setup Wizard do?
expectedAnswer: The conversational Setup Wizard (Loom Copilot) interviews you about your tenant, subscriptions, boundary, and capacity sizing, renders a live .bicepparam preview, and deploys via a self-hosted Azure MCP server inside your Admin Plane.

question: Which data catalog does Loom use in an IL5 deployment?
expectedAnswer: DoD IL5 deployments use Apache Atlas on AKS as the catalog (Purview is the primary catalog for GCC-High/IL4; Commercial/GCC use Databricks Unity Catalog with a Purview overlay).

question: Do Loom reports need a Power BI workspace?
expectedAnswer: No. The default report backend is the Loom-native renderer, which queries the bound Azure Analysis Services tabular model with DAX — no Power BI or Fabric workspace. The Power BI embed path is strictly opt-in via LOOM_BI_BACKEND=powerbi.

question: How do I verify the Console after a deployment finishes?
expectedAnswer: Open the Console URL and sign in with an Entra identity that is a member of the Loom Admins group. You should see the home hub, an empty Workspaces pane, the Catalog, and a green Monitoring hub; first-run setup lives at /setup.
